parameters.content:
## FleetCheck — Pre-Trip Driver Safety System

A FMCSA-aligned pre-trip check-in system for commercial drivers. 
Combines AI-powered risk scoring, automatic supervisor alerts, 
and a dispatcher dashboard into one self-hosted package.

**What's included:**
- n8n workflow (AI scoring, flag detection, alert routing)
- Flask web app (index.html check-in form + dispatcher dashboard)
- SQLite audit trail of every submission
- Gmail + ntfy push notification alerts

---

**Requirements**
- Windows or Linux PC (8GB RAM minimum for AI model)
- Python 3.9+
- Docker Desktop
- ngrok account (free tier works, paid for static URL)
- Gmail account with App Password enabled
- ntfy app on dispatcher's phone (free)

---

**Setup Instructions**

**1. Install Python dependencies**
Download the Flask app files 
app.py, templates folder) and run:
    pip install flask requests

**2. Install Docker Desktop**
Download from docker.com and create an account.
Then run:
    docker volume create n8n_data

    docker run -d \
      --name n8n \
      --restart unless-stopped \
      -p 5678:5678 \
      -e GENERIC_TIMEZONE="America/Chicago" \
      -e N8N_ENFORCE_SETTINGS_FILE_PERMISSIONS=true \
      -e N8N_RUNNERS_ENABLED=true \
      -v n8n_data:/home/node/.n8n \
      docker.n8n.io/n8nio/n8n

Open http://localhost:5678 and create your n8n account.

**3. Import this workflow**
In n8n go to: top menu → Import from file → select the 
downloaded workflow JSON.

**4. Configure Gmail alerts**
- Create a Gmail App Password at myaccount.google.com → 
  Security → App Passwords
- In n8n open the Send Email node and enter your Gmail 
  address and App Password
- Set the To field to your dispatcher's email address

**5. Configure ntfy notifications**
- Download the ntfy app on your dispatcher's phone (iOS/Android)
- Choose a private channel name e.g. fleet-alerts-yourcompany









- In n8n open the ntfy HTTP Request node and update the URL 
  to: https://ntfy.sh/your-channel-name
- Subscribe to the same channel in the ntfy app

**6. Install Ollama (local AI)**
Download from ollama.com then run:
    ollama pull mistral

**7. Install ngrok**
Download from ngrok.com and sign up. Then run:
    ngrok config add-authtoken YOUR_TOKEN
    ngrok http 5000

For a permanent URL (paid plan):
    ngrok http 5000 --url yourname.ngrok.app

**8. Start the app**
    python app.py
**9. Publish the workflow**
In n8n, open the workflow and click Publish in the top right.
**10. Test it**
Open your ngrok URL in a browser and submit a test check-in.
Check http://localhost:5000/dashboard to see results.


---


**How It Works**
Driver opens the ngrok URL on any device → fills out 
pre-trip check-in → AI analyzes responses → dispatcher 
receives instant alert if flagged → all submissions logged 
to local SQLite database → dispatcher reviews via dashboard.















---

**Disclaimer**
This is a voluntary safety support tool designed to assist 
fleet operators in identifying potential driver fitness concerns. 
It does not constitute a medical assessment, legal certification, 
or guarantee of regulatory compliance. Final dispatch authority 
rests with the fleet operator. All FMCSA regulations remain the 
responsibility of the carrier.

## Additional Configurations

Dispatcher Dashboard can be Accessed at: http://localhost:5000/dashboard
It shows all submissions, scores, flags, and hard stops.
The data can be exported to CSV for compliance records.
The page auto-refreshes every 60 seconds.

    **When a driver passes an assessment there are no red flags and so there is no notification that is sent.*
    **Be sure to setup the HTTP Request node to configure ntfy notifications**















    







**Be sure to configure the "Send an Email" node by using a 
    Google account with app_password, 
    see https://support.google.com/accounts/answer/185833?hl=en# 
    for help with setting up those credentials for the node.**


 
To test the system, have all previous steps completed, and apps and scripts running
modify the app.py webhook to reflect testing and then visit the ngrok URL, 
a badge number like "Badge ID TEST001" which can be used for testing without affecting real records.
